evidence.title: Courthouse News report on unsealed Epstein note
evidence.source_description: Courthouse News Service article by Josh Russell; page text extracted with local HTML parser on 2026-06-30 UTC.
evidence.relevance: Corroborates public availability and unsealing on May 6, 2026; describes the note as a purported suicide note, identifies court/litigation path, and includes source-chain targets for handwritten note and NYT unsealing request.

evidence.title: AP/PBS report on Epstein note release
evidence.source_description: PBS NewsHour publication of Associated Press report by Michael R. Sisak and Larry Neumeister; page text extracted with local HTML parser on 2026-06-30 UTC.
evidence.relevance: Supports public availability before May 8, 2026, court release chain, note text and caption attribution; also preserves adverse caveats on unclear authorship, DOJ files absence, suicide-watch denial, and custody context.

evidence.title: FOX local report with full text of purported Epstein note
evidence.source_description: FOX 4 Dallas-Fort Worth / FOX Local article by Catherine Stoddard; page text extracted with local HTML parser on 2026-06-30 UTC.
evidence.relevance: Provides a concise public article dated May 6, 2026 saying a federal judge unsealed the purported suicide note and reproducing the apparent full text; supports public availability and intent characterization while preserving hedging ('may have been written').

evidence.title: SDNY May 6, 2026 order granting NYT request to unseal alleged Epstein note
evidence.source_description: Official U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York PDF, United States v. Nicholas Tartaglione, Case No. 16-CR-832 (KMK), order by Judge Kenneth M. Karas dated May 6, 2026. Downloaded during defendant argument; local PDF size 128,992 bytes; local SHA-256 7225441cb802084e2b9485ebc2c6d246fbfa08e109fe650cb14f445dc4745ca0. PDF text was extracted by the web PDF text view because local pdftotext and pip/pypdf were unavailable.
evidence.relevance: Primary official source for what the court did and did not decide: the order grants unsealing of an alleged suicide note purportedly authored by Epstein, identifies the NYT request and Tartaglione source chain, and directs docketing/unsealing, but frames the item as alleged/purported and does not authenticate authorship, chain of custody, or intent.

evidence.title: AP handwriting analysis: common authorship suggested but Epstein not definitively identified
evidence.source_description: Associated Press article retrieved through web search/fetch during defendant argument on 2026-06-30 UTC. Title: 'Handwriting on newly released note matches one found after Epstein's death, experts tell AP.'
evidence.relevance: Strongest adverse source on authorship. It reports handwriting experts found or appeared to find common authorship between the released note and a grievance note found after Epstein's death, but preserves that no expert definitively said Epstein wrote the notes and that authenticated Epstein handwriting samples were lacking; it also supports the defense's intent distinction because the comparison note was a non-suicide grievance note.

evidence.title: AP report: DOJ says purported Epstein note was unauthenticated and first seen by DOJ
evidence.source_description: Associated Press article retrieved through web search/fetch during defendant argument on 2026-06-30 UTC. Search result exposed full article text. Article title: 'Epstein cellmate says he found a suicide note. Justice Department says it's seeing it for first time.'
evidence.relevance: Adverse-to-plaintiff source from AP preserving the Justice Department's statement that the note was not authenticated and was being seen by DOJ for the first time; also preserves source-chain facts tying the note to Tartaglione and sealed attorney-client proceedings rather than DOJ death files.
